_comment: Canonical decision engine contract for DenkKern V1 demo — case SH-2024-0042. This single file powers all 8 operational screens. Do NOT hardcode any UI values; derive all display data from this file. See docs/strategy/ux-v1-information-architecture.md for screen contract.

industry_templates.label: Shipbuilding & Heavy Manufacturing
industry_templates.description: Optimised for naval, merchant vessel, and offshore platform construction. Long lead times, critical material specs, high contract penalty exposure.

industry_templates.label: Food Manufacturing & Import Operations
industry_templates.description: Perishable goods, regulatory compliance, shelf-life constraints. Emphasises speed of resolution and regulatory risk.

industry_templates.label: Healthcare & Medical Products
industry_templates.description: Medical device and pharmaceutical supply. Zero tolerance for stockout risk. Regulatory traceability required for every substitution.

industry_templates.label: Custom
industry_templates.description: No pre-configuration. Build your evaluation framework, approval chain, and thresholds from scratch.

lena_configuration._comment: Active Lena configuration, derived from shipbuilding_heavy_manufacturing template and reviewed by operator during Smart Setup.
responsibilities: Monitor active shipments and surface delay risk before production impact
Generate scenario options and rank by weighted evaluation framework
Route recommendations through correct approval chain

shipment_context.shipment_name: Marine-Grade Steel Plates DH36 — MV Nordstern

material.name: Marine-Grade Steel Plates DH36
material.specification: DH36 — DNV GL certified, hull-quality shipbuilding steel
material.substitution_notes: DH36 spec is required for Hull Section 4 structural integrity per project contract. S355J2 may be used for non-structural sections only (max 15% of volume).

vessel.name: MV Nordstern
vessel.current_location: Rotterdam anchorage zone, waiting for berth

schedule.delay_cause_primary: Rotterdam port congestion — berth unavailability 9+ days
schedule.delay_cause_secondary: North Sea weather advisory — Beaufort 7–8 forecast June 16–19

disruption_context.summary: MV Nordstern delayed 24 days due to Rotterdam port congestion. Hull Section 4 production stops in 3 days if no action taken.

risk_signals.description: Terminal congestion — 9+ day berth delay. 14 vessels ahead in queue.
Beaufort 7–8 forecast June 16–19. Transit likely delayed by additional 2–3 days.
DH36 steel stock: 12 metric tonnes remaining. Covers 3 days of production. No local backup stock identified.

eta_scenarios.label: Worst case — extended congestion + mechanical issue

constraints: DH36 specification cannot be substituted for structural plates
Supplier must hold DNV GL certification
Delivery must arrive by 2026-06-24 to avoid production stop
Any cost above €250,000 requires VP Operations approval

organization_rules.label: Production Continuity Override
organization_rules.description: When production stop is imminent (< 5 days) on a strategic contract, production continuity score weight increases from 0.45 to 0.60. Financial weight decreases to 0.10.

organization_rules.label: Critical Material Lock
organization_rules.description: When material is flagged critical and no substitution is possible, options that do not deliver the specified material are automatically disqualified.

organization_rules.label: Approval Gate
organization_rules.description: Any recommended action with cost above the approval_threshold_eur must be routed to the level-1 approver before execution.

scenarios._comment: SCENARIO = what may happen to the shipment. Not the same as ACTION (what we can do).
scenarios.label: Base Delay — Port Congestion + Weather
scenarios.description: MV Nordstern remains at Rotterdam anchorage. Congestion clears in 9 days. Weather adds 3 days transit. Vessel arrives Hamburg July 4.

scenarios.label: Optimistic — Congestion Clears Early
scenarios.description: Emergency berth allocation reduces wait to 3 days. Favourable weather. Vessel arrives Hamburg June 22.

scenarios.label: Worst Case — Extended Delay + Mechanical Issue
scenarios.description: Vessel develops mechanical fault during berth wait. Repairs + congestion delay arrival to July 15.

actions._comment: ACTION = what we can do to mitigate. Evaluated against all scenarios. Not the same as SCENARIO.
actions.label: Air Freight — ArcelorMittal Fos-sur-Mer
actions.description: Charter air freight of 240 MT DH36 plates from ArcelorMittal's certified stock in Fos-sur-Mer, France. Cargo aircraft (AN-124) from Marseille to Hamburg Airport. Ground transport to shipyard same day.
actions.feasibility: confirmed — ArcelorMittal stock verified, AN-124 available Marseille June 15

actions.label: Road Freight — ThyssenKrupp Duisburg Warehouse
actions.description: Emergency road transport from ThyssenKrupp backup stock in Duisburg. TIR-certified flatbed convoy. Estimated 18-hour road time Hamburg arrival June 17.
actions.feasibility: conditional — ThyssenKrupp stock partially available (180 MT confirmed, 60 MT on hold pending internal release)

actions.label: Split Order — S355J2 Local + Wait for DH36
actions.description: Source 36 MT of S355J2 steel (permitted for non-structural sections only) from Hamburg local stockist for frames 12–14. Wait for original DH36 shipment for frames 15–18. Reduces but does not eliminate downtime.
actions.meets_spec_notes: S355J2 only approved for 15% of volume (non-structural). Structural plates (frames 15–18) still require original DH36 delivery.
actions.feasibility: confirmed for S355J2 portion — DH36 portion depends on shipment arrival (SCN-001: July 4)

actions.label: Wait for Original Shipment
actions.description: Take no action. Wait for MV Nordstern to arrive. Production line stops June 18 for estimated 16 days under base scenario.
actions.feasibility: confirmed — but production stop is certain under base and worst-case scenarios

engine_output._comment: RECOMMENDATION = what DenkKern suggests. Derived from scoring all actions against all scenarios under the active evaluation framework and organization rules. The recommendation is NOT a scenario and NOT an action — it is the engine's ranked output.

recommendation.label: Air Freight — ArcelorMittal Fos-sur-Mer
recommendation.approval_required_reason: Action cost €480,000 exceeds approval threshold of €250,000

production_continuity.rationale: Arrives June 16 — 2 days before production stop. 97% delivery confidence. Production stop fully averted.

financial_impact.rationale: Action cost €480K is high. Offset by €7M total exposure averted. Net saving justifies cost under ORG-R-001 weight override.

customer_commitment.rationale: Production resumes June 17. Naval vessel delivery deadline October 15 preserved. No customer commitment breach.

supplier_relations.rationale: Original Nippon Steel order retained. ArcelorMittal emergency order has minimal impact on existing supplier relationships.

production_continuity.rationale: Arrives June 17 — 1 day before stop. 76% confidence introduces 24% probability of production stop. Partial DH36 shortfall (60 MT on hold) creates further uncertainty.

financial_impact.rationale: Low action cost €62K. Below approval threshold.

customer_commitment.rationale: Conditional on full stock release. If 60 MT hold not resolved, production partially impacted. Customer commitment at moderate risk.

supplier_relations.rationale: ThyssenKrupp is an established backup supplier. Emergency order is within normal operating relationship.

production_continuity.rationale: Partial solution only. Non-structural frames proceed; structural frames (15–18) blocked until DH36 arrives July 4. 16-day partial stop remains.

financial_impact.rationale: Low direct cost €95K. Partial downtime cost unavoidable. Better than full wait.

customer_commitment.rationale: Partial work continues. Delivery deadline at moderate risk depending on rescheduling feasibility.

supplier_relations.rationale: Original Nippon order untouched. Minimal supplier impact.

score_breakdown.disqualification_reason: ORG-R-002: Critical material flag active and substitution not possible. Waiting for delayed shipment with 16-day production stop violates Production Continuity rule on strategic contract.

production_continuity.rationale: Production stops June 18. 16-day downtime under base scenario.

financial_impact.rationale: Zero action cost. But €7.48M total exposure (downtime + penalties) makes this the worst financial outcome.

customer_commitment.rationale: Naval vessel delivery at high risk. 16-day slip may cascade into delivery deadline breach.

supplier_relations.rationale: No disruption to Nippon Steel relationship. Original order untouched.

rules_triggered.label: Production Continuity Override
rules_triggered.effect: Production continuity weight increased from 0.45 → 0.60. Financial weight decreased from 0.25 → 0.10.

rules_triggered.label: Critical Material Lock
rules_triggered.effect: ACT-004 (Wait) disqualified. Full DH36 substitution not available. Production stop under base scenario is certain.

rules_triggered.label: Approval Gate
rules_triggered.effect: ACT-001 requires Level-1 approval from VP Operations (Mark Hoffmann) before execution.

explanation_trace.output: 3 risk signals loaded: port_congestion (HIGH), weather_advisory (MEDIUM), inventory_status (HIGH)
3 ETA scenarios generated: SCN-001 (p=0.61), SCN-002 (p=0.14), SCN-003 (p=0.25)
ORG-R-001 triggered: days_until_production_stop=3, contract_type=strategic. Weights overridden.
ORG-R-002 triggered: critical_material_flag=true, substitution_possible=false. ACT-004 disqualified.
4 candidate actions generated. 3 eligible after rule disqualification.
Scores computed with overridden weights: ACT-001=0.912, ACT-002=0.774, ACT-003=0.581, ACT-004=0.093 (disqualified)
Highest eligible score: ACT-001 (0.912). Recommendation confirmed.
ACT-001 cost €480K > threshold €250K. ORG-R-003 triggered. Approval required: Mark Hoffmann (VP Operations).
Execution validation checklist generated for ACT-001. 6 checkpoints defined.
Projected outcome: production resumes June 17. Net saving vs wait: €7.0M.

approval_routing._comment: Derived from ORG-R-003 + decision_constraints.approval_chain. Cost > threshold → approval required.
approval_routing.approval_required_reason: ACT-001 cost €480,000 exceeds Lena configuration approval threshold of €250,000
approval_routing.override_notes: No override permitted for actions > threshold on strategic contracts (ORG-R-003)

execution_validation._comment: Checklist generated by execution_planner agent. All items must be confirmed before execution is marked complete.

checklist.label: ArcelorMittal stock confirmation
checklist.description: Confirm 240 MT DH36 DNV GL certified plates physically available and reserved at Fos-sur-Mer.

checklist.label: AN-124 charter booking confirmed
checklist.description: Antonov AN-124 charter from Marseille Provence Airport confirmed, cargo manifest approved, slot reserved.

checklist.label: DNV GL certificate validated
checklist.description: DNV GL certification documents for the specific batch received and cross-checked against hull specification requirements.

checklist.label: Hamburg unloading crew and equipment confirmed
checklist.description: Hamburg Airport heavy-load crane and flatbed transport to shipyard confirmed for June 16 arrival.

checklist.label: Customs pre-clearance filed
checklist.description: Import customs pre-clearance for EN goods from France filed. Broker instructed. Expected fast-track (EU internal).

checklist.label: Production line readiness confirmed
checklist.description: Hull Section 4 production line cleared and ready for DH36 receipt on June 16. Crew on standby.

execution_validation.execution_cannot_proceed_reason: Approval pending (ORG-R-003). Blocking checklist items EXV-001, EXV-002, EXV-003 not yet confirmed.

projected_outcome.original_shipment_status: Continue — MV Nordstern proceeds to Hamburg for future projects. Original order retained.

mock_actual_outcome._comment: Pre-seeded mock outcome for demo — simulates what actually happened after execution.
mock_actual_outcome.lessons_learned: Early signal detection (9 days before production stop) was critical. Booking cutoff enforced 10-hour decision window.
mock_actual_outcome.outcome_label: SUCCESS — Production continuity preserved. €7.0M net saving delivered.

derived_views._comment: These views are derived from engine_output fields. No UI values are hardcoded here — all values reference engine_output above.

lena_action_queue.note: Approval required after decision accepted (cost > threshold). Mark Hoffmann, VP Operations. Deadline 18:00 today.

graph_structures._comment: Graph-ready structures for visual representation. These are display-only — no real graph DB is required for V1 demo.

organization_graph.description: Org structure as a directed graph. Nodes = people/roles. Edges = reporting lines and approval paths.

decision_model_graph.description: Decision model as a weighted graph. Nodes = evaluation dimensions. Edges = weight relationships under active rules.

decision_lifecycle_graph.description: Decision lifecycle as a directed state machine. Nodes = lifecycle states. Edges = transitions.
